pi_items.source: Direct inspection of Chinook SQLite database schema using sqlite3

SQL query execution: SELECT g.Name, SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity) FROM InvoiceLine il JOIN Track t ON il.TrackId = t.TrackId JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId GROUP BY g.Name ORDER BY Revenue DESC; Result: Rock is rank 1 with $826.65

SQL query execution on Chinook_Sqlite.sqlite database at https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database/raw/master/ChinookDatabase/DataSources/Chinook_Sqlite.sqlite. Query: SELECT SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity) FROM InvoiceLine il JOIN Track t ON il.TrackId = t.TrackId JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId WHERE g.Name='Rock' GROUP BY g.Name; Result: 826.65
Configuration parameter in task.json for verifier.py; matches typical floating-point tolerance for currency calculations
Environment installation script (install_libreoffice_base.sh) downloads database to this exact path from GitHub

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the Chinook database, a real public sample database downloaded from https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database. The data is based on iTunes Library exports and realistic customer information. The expected query result is verified: Rock is the top music genre by total sales revenue with exactly $826.65 in revenue. All three required tables (Genre, Track, InvoiceLine) exist in the database. The query correctly joins these tables via TrackId and GenreId foreign keys, uses SUM(UnitPrice * Quantity) for revenue calculation, and orders by revenue in descending order.
